steps.title: Identify the core measurement being tested
steps.content: The key signal is the word incidence—which refers to the rate or frequency of occurrence—paired with the example of “day” as “the fifth most commonly used noun.” These details establish that the passage measures how widespread or common a word is in English.

steps.title: Match the word to the frequency test
steps.content: C) pervasive means widely distributed or present everywhere, which is precisely what the passage tests for in word corpora. The other three choices (assertive, credible, profound) describe unrelated qualities—boldness, trustworthiness, and depth—not word prevalence. That matches choice C — C is correct.
